Cc: procurement@scoutai.com

Subject: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the low-latency streaming looked very promising. We ...
text:
From: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@scoutai.com
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the low-latency streaming looked very promising. We pushed the first few traces through overnight and found two things that matter for our production PoC:

- We need a consistent time-to-first-token (TTFT) under ~150ms for short-turn utterances (20-40 tokens) to keep downstream speaker-turn detection reliable.
- For longer inference sessions we require a sustained token emission of ~60 tokens/sec for the steady-state agent workload.

Given our current usage profile (bursts up to 120 concurrent streams, median request length 150 tokens), our cost envelope doesn't allow us to move to a fully Dedicated cluster right now. Can Re...

Subject: Pilot: MAP and concrete acceptance tests

Logan —

Thanks for the demo last week. The execs at NovaHealth want a short, measurable mutual action plan we can use to greenlight a pilot and make a close decision. High level asks from our side: 

- Performance: p95 inference latency <= 200ms for 512-token clinical prompts (baseline traffic ~10 qps).
- Reliability: no more than one incident requiring manual failover during a 2-week trial.
- Functional: named-entity extraction F1 >= 0.90 on the lab-results dataset (we'll provide a 500-record labeled sample).
- Security/compliance: data-in-transit + at-rest with retention policy controls, audit logs for requests.

Can you send a MAP that maps these to concrete accepta...

Hi Logan —

We've seen a handful of requests return an empty string body (200 OK but no text) from /v1/generate over the past 36 hours. This is hitting our checkout microservice and is causing brief feature regressions for a small percentage of users.

What we've collected so far:
- Examples: request IDs listed in attached api-logs-2026-01-12.txt (lines 332, 451, 716)
- One attached screenshot showing the response body blank \"\"
- No visible spike in latency; errors are not 4xx/5xx — just empty content with normal tokens count header

Can your team confirm whether this is a known issue, or provide next steps for triage? We're happy to provide any more traces.

Thanks,
Samira Khan
SRE, FastRetail

We've attached our proposed redlines to the DPA and a simple DR flow diagram to show the narrow scope.

High-level ask: allow encrypted backup copies for DR for up to 120 days post-deletion, with access restricted to restore-only operations, logged and limited to named custodians. We also included a request to permit cross-border replication to our EU failover region (Ida Cloud) for resilience.

F...
text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Hi Sara —

Following up from our kickoff: attached is an initial matrix that maps our control-plane/data-plane attachment points, expected egress destinations, and suggested firewall/NAT patterns for the private VPC deployment. This is v1 (vpc-egress-attachment-matrix-v1.xlsx).

High level:
- Control plane (Redwood managed) will terminate in the customer VPC via a dedicated control-plane endpoint in the management subnet.
- Data-plane inference traffic will stay in-customer (tenant subnets) and only contact Redwood endpoints for model metadata/rollouts and telemetry when allowed.
- We ...
